purpose: Within-document register-discontinuity locator: slides a deterministic sentence-anchored window over ONE text, measures the stylometric distance between adjacent windows (stdlib function-word + char-3/4/5-gram + sentence-shape features), and emits the internal span boundaries where the register shifts most — each as a stylistic_shift / register_discontinuity boundary with character offsets and an ordinal band. NEVER emits an author count, a "different authors" claim, or any authorship inference. The "never an authorship claim" guarantee is MECHANICAL: FORBIDDEN_RESULT_KEYS frozenset + FORBIDDEN_SUBSTRINGS tuple + recursive assert_no_authorship() guard (raises AuthorshipClaimError) called immediately before build_output, backed by a BAND_VOCAB whitelist (none / slight_shift / moderate_shift / marked_shift; plus unscaled for the zero-dispersion zero-MAD case where ordinal severity is unscalable; no "author" token) and an available:false policy_refused refusal path. M1 lens = stylometric cosine distance (stdlib, model-free, deterministic). M2 embedding boundary lens is a POC-gated --lens embedding seam (lazy-import + fail-loud missing_dependency), not in this build. Single input only; cross-document comparison is un-expressible by CLI construction (no --reference / --compare / --manifest flag). Posture: descriptive / no-verdict / anti-Goodhart; calibration_status PROVISIONAL.
use_when: You have a SINGLE text and want to know where inside it the style shifts most (internal register-discontinuity boundaries with character offsets and verbatim excerpts)
You want to locate likely edit seams, genre switches, or mode transitions within one document without making any authorship claim
The text has at least min_windows (default 3) usable sentence-anchored windows
do_not_use_when: You want an authorship / "different authors" / author-count determination — the surface refuses this by construction; a register boundary is NOT an authorship boundary
You have two or more documents to compare cross-document (there is no second input; the cross-document question is un-expressible through this CLI)
The text is below the window floor (< ~30 words / < 3 usable windows)
You want calibrated absolute thresholds — bands are within-document MAD-relative (median + k*MAD), not absolute calibrated cuts; calibration_status is always provisional

inputs.text: a single --input path (.txt / .md) or positional arg, or '-' for stdin; NO --reference / --compare / --manifest flag exists
inputs.knobs: --window-sentences (default 5), --stride-sentences (default 2), --peak-k (default 2.5), --min-windows (default 3), --lens stylometric (M1) | embedding (gated M2 seam, not in this build)

compute.cost_note: M1 pure-stdlib stylometric lens (function-word + char-3/4/5-gram + sentence-shape features; within-document z-score; adjacent cosine distance; MAD peak detection); no models, no numpy, no API, deterministic. The M2 embedding/semantic lens is a POC-gated --lens embedding seam (lazy-import + fail-loud missing_dependency), not in this build.

examples.description: Within-document register-discontinuity boundaries for a single text file
Narrower windows and higher peak threshold

references: Overview of PAN 2026: Voight-Kampff Generative AI Detection, Text Watermarking, Multi-Author Writing Style Analysis, Generative Plagiarism Detection, and Reasoning Trajectory Detection (arXiv:2602.09147): https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.09147
